title: Generate requirements for a battery-powered handheld vacuum
stakeholder_needs:
Generate requirements for a battery-powered handheld vacuum
    cleaner for home use. It should run for at least 20 minutes,
    weigh under 1.5 kg, and meet EU safety standards.
analysis: Stakeholders include: primary users (homeowners/renters needing quick cleanups), secondary users (family members with varying strength/height), and purchasers (cost/value expectations). Key needs: portability (under 1.5 kg), sufficient runtime (>=20 min), effective suction and debris pickup for common home surfaces, easy emptying/cleaning, low noise, fast charging, acceptable battery life degradation over time, and ergonomic handling. Regulatory stakeholders: EU market compliance (CE marking; Low Voltage Directive likely if mains charger, EMC, RoHS, REACH; plus battery transport/UN 38.3 and IEC/EN safety standards such as EN 60335-1/-2-2). Manufacturing/support stakeholders: reliability, serviceability, replaceable filters/battery, warranty, and documentation. Requirements must balance performance vs weight and runtime (battery capacity, motor power, airflow), and include measurable verification methods and acceptance criteria.
clarification_questions: What floor types and debris should it be optimized for (e.g., hard floors only vs also carpets/rugs; dust, pet hair, crumbs, liquids)?
What charging expectations are required (USB-C vs dock, max charge time, replaceable battery, mains voltage/plug markets within EU)?
What target price range and noise limit (dB(A)) should it meet?

messages.content:
Generate requirements for a battery-powered handheld vacuum
    cleaner for home use. It should run for at least 20 minutes,
    weigh under 1.5 kg, and meet EU safety standards.

Stakeholders include: primary users (homeowners/renters needing quick cleanups), secondary users (family members with varying strength/height), and purchasers (cost/value expectations). Key needs: portability (under 1.5 kg), sufficient runtime (>=20 min), effective suction and debris pickup for common home surfaces, easy emptying/cleaning, low noise, fast charging, acceptable battery life degradation over time, and ergonomic handling. Regulatory stakeholders: EU market compliance (CE marking; Low Voltage Directive likely if mains charger, EMC, RoHS, REACH; plus battery transport/UN 38.3 and IEC/EN safety standards such as EN 60335-1/-2-2). Manufacturing/support stakeholders: reliability, serviceability, replaceable filters/battery, warranty, and documentation. Requirements must balance performance vs weight and runtime (battery capacity, motor power, airflow), and include measurable verification methods and acceptance criteria.
